summary: This chunk details the second purification for a Divine Vision, including fasting requirements (12 days bread and water), prayers before receiving the Eucharist, and specific divine names to invoke. It outlines the 'Separation' phase where the practitioner isolates themselves, recites psalms, and uses lists of God's names for protection and cleansing. The text concludes with instructions for the Completion of the Work involving a bed of hay on ashes surrounded by written names.
key_passages: Mundato igitur et macerato corpore volentis videre celeste palacium ipsum mundissimum esse iubemus
Tu, Domine Iesu Christe, salvator omnium, qui pro me, miserimo peccatore... voluisti corpus tuum salubriter immolare
Agla, Monhon, Tetragramaton, Ely Deus, Ocleiste, Amphynethon, Lamyara, Ianemyer, Sadyon, Hely, Horlon, Porrenthimon
Fothon, celi et terre conditor; Lethellete, qui celum super altitudinem nubium extendisti
Die vero Iovis sumo mane dicat ut dixerat in predictis et tunc super cineres de fece mundatos de feno cubile faciat
